TikTok和总部设在美国的全国音乐出版商协会(NMPA)签署了一份多年的许可协议。
NMPA的成员包括所有三大音乐出版商，外加世界上最大的印度公司。根据NMPA的说法，这笔交易“解释了TikTok过去对音乐作品的使用”，并建立了“前瞻性合作伙伴关系”。
这一措辞的第一部分表明，TikTok已经同意向NMPA支付过去未经许可使用其成员作品的费用。
第二部分(关于“前瞻性伙伴关系”的BIT)将使NMPA成员能够选择加入一项具有追溯力的许可协议，该协议自2020年5月1日起生效。
这笔交易似乎结束了NMPA和TikTok之间日益加剧的紧张关系-前党公开(非常故意地)向这家字节跳动拥有的公司施加了巨大的压力。
2019年10月，NMPA呼吁对TikTok进行“审查”，称该视频分享平台“一直违反美国版权法以及词曲作者和音乐出版商的权利”。
据英国“金融时报”报道，4月份，音乐出版商通过NMPA正式威胁要起诉TikTok。
当时，NMPA首席执行官大卫·伊斯雷特(David Israite)告诉英国“金融时报”，起诉TikTok侵犯版权“可能是未来的一步”。
不出所料，以色列人在宣布今天的新闻时所说的话要更亲切一些。
他说：“我们很高兴通过TikTok找到一条前进的道路，让词曲作者和出版商受益，并为他们的工作提供关键的补偿。”
“音乐是TikTok等应用程序的重要组成部分，这些应用程序将歌曲与表达融合在一起，在普及新音乐的同时，也赋予了经典歌曲新的生命。
“这份协议尊重创作者的工作，并为他们为平台做出的必要贡献提供了一种支付方式。”
我们很高兴能与NMPA合作，将他们的成员公司带到这个平台上，帮助数亿人发现和享受他们的歌曲。“。
TikTok全球音乐主管Ole Obermann表示：“TikTok很自豪能与音乐出版商和词曲作者合作，让艺术家和歌曲被发现，并支持收入机会。
我们很高兴能与NMPA合作，将他们的成员公司带到这个平台上，帮助数亿人发现和享受他们的歌曲。
我们期待着继续与词曲作者合作，帮助他们将TikTok作为一个强大的创新渠道，通过独特的创作和参与形式接触到全球观众。“。
“这份协议尊重创作者的工作，并为他们为平台做出的必要贡献提供了一种支付方式。”
昨天(7月22日)有消息称，TikTok已经与总部位于巴黎的Believe签署了一份多年的分销和营销协议。全球音乐业务
note_en:
TikTok and the US-based National Music Publishers’ Association (NMPA) have inked a multi-year licensing agreement.
  According to the NMPA – whose members include all three major music publishers, plus the world’s biggest indies – the deal “accounts for TikTok’s past use of musical works” as well as setting up a “forward-looking partnership”.
 The first part of that phrasing suggests that TikTok has agreed to pay the NMPA a fee for unlicensed usage of its members’ works in the past.
  The second part (the bit about the “forward-looking partnership”) will give NMPA members the ability to opt-in to a licensing agreement which is effective retroactively as of May 1, 2020.
 The deal appears to have brought to a close mounting tensions between the NMPA and TikTok – with the former party publicly (and very deliberately) heaping pressure on the Bytedance-owned company.
 In October 2019, the NMPA called for the “scrutiny” of TikTok, writing that the video-sharing platform has “consistently violated US copyright law and the rights of songwriters and music publishers”.
 And in April, according to a  Financial Times report, music publishers – via the NMPA – officially threatened to sue TikTok.
 At the time, David Israelite, CEO of the NMPA, told the FT that a lawsuit against TikTok for copyright infringement was a “likely future step”.
 As you’d expect, Israelite’s quote announcing today’s news is rather more cordial.
 He said: “We are pleased to find a way forward with TikTok which benefits songwriters and publishers and offers them critical compensation for their work.
 “Music is an important part of apps like TikTok which merge songs with expression and popularize new music while also giving new life to classic songs.
 “This agreement respects the work of creators and gives them a way to be paid for their essential contributions to the platform.”
 “We’re excited to partner with the NMPA to bring their member companies on to the platform and help hundreds of millions of people discover and enjoy their songs.”
 Ole Obermann, Global Head of Music at TikTok, said: “TikTok is proud to partner with music publishers and songwriters to enable artist and song discovery, and support revenue opportunities.
 “We’re excited to partner with the NMPA to bring their member companies on to the platform and help hundreds of millions of people discover and enjoy their songs.
 “We look forward to continuing to work with songwriters to help them use TikTok as a powerful and innovative channel to reach a global audience through a unique format of creation and engagement.”
 “This agreement respects the work of creators and gives them a way to be paid for their essential contributions to the platform.”
 The new deal  follows yesterday’s news (July 22), that TikTok has signed a multi-year distribution and marketing agreement with Paris-headquartered Believe. Music Business Worldwide
title: TikTok和全国音乐出版商协会签署了许可协议，表明TikTok已同意为过去未经许可使用会员作品付费
title_en: TikTok and the National Music Publishers' Association sign licensing agreement that suggests TikTok has agreed to pay for unlicensed past use of members' works

note:
周五，Index的三位主要编辑Attila Tóth-Szenesi、Veronika Munk和János Haász已开始终止他们在Index的雇佣关系，随后有70多名记者在该网站工作。
他们做出决定的原因是Index.hu Zrt董事会主席拉兹洛·博多莱(LászlóBodolai)。已经解雇了前总编辑斯兹博尔茨迟钝，并拒绝恢复他，以确保索引的独立性和未来，尽管该网站的编辑人员的要求。周三，工作人员一致站在被解雇的主编一边，称博多莱的决定是不可接受的。
多年来，我们一直在说，索引的独立运营有两个条件：对我们发布的内容或我们员工的结构和组成没有外部影响。解雇沉闷的施博尔茨违反了我们的第二个条件。他的解雇是对我们员工组成的明显干预，我们只能将其视为公开试图向Index.hu施压。
在这种情况下，根据博多莱的决定，编辑委员会认为独立运营的条件不再存在，并已开始终止他们的雇用。
因主编斯兹博尔茨离职而递交辞呈的工作人员名单沉闷：
和我们欧盟分部EUROLOGUS的团队(阿拉托·拉兹洛，福尔克·格奥尔基，马尔顿·巴拉兹，Szűcságnes，扎兰·埃兹特)
note_en:
On Friday, the three leading editors of Index, Attila Tóth-Szenesi, Veronika Munk, and János Haász have initiated the termination of their employment at Index, followed by more than 70 journalists working at the site.
  The reason behind their decision was that László Bodolai, the president of the board of Index.hu Zrt. has  fired former editor-in-chief Szabolcs Dull and has  refused to reinstate him in order to secure the independence and future of Index despite the request from the site's editorial staff. On Wednesday, members of the staff have unanimously taken a stand by the dismissed editor-in-chief, describing Bodolai's decision as  unacceptable.
  For years, we've been saying that there are two conditions for the independent operation of Index: that there be no external influence on the content we publish or the structure and composition of our staff. Firing Szabolcs Dull has violated our second condition. His dismissal is a clear interference in the composition of our staff,   and we cannot regard it any other way but as an overt attempt to apply pressure on Index.hu.
  Under these circumstances, following Bodolai's decision, the editorial board deemed that the conditions for independent operation are no longer in place and have initiated the termination of their employment.
    The list of staff members who handed in their resignations because of the termination of editor-in-chief Szabolcs Dull:
                                                                                                                                                  and the team of our EU subsection Eurológus (Arató László, Folk György, Márton Balázs, Szűcs Ágnes, Zalán Eszter)
title: 指数编辑委员会和70多名员工辞职
title_en: Editorial board of Index and more than 70 staff members resign

note:
根据“华尔街日报”的达娜·马蒂奥利和卡拉·隆巴多的一份最新报告，亚马逊一直在利用创业投资过程来帮助其制造自己的竞争产品。
华尔街日报采访了数十名初创公司创始人、投资者和顾问，他们表示，亚马逊与他们的公司会面或投资，但后来却开发了自己的产品，与规模较小的公司直接竞争。华尔街日报发现，亚马逊制造的产品往往会在竞争中一败涂地。
“华尔街日报”发现了几个亚马逊投资导致内部产品开发的例子。团购网站LivingSocial告诉“华尔街日报”，在亚马逊收购该公司30%的股份后，它开始向公司索取大量数据，雇佣员工，并联系LivingSocial的客户，以提供更优惠的交易。
在另一个例子中，Alexa基金的投资者购买了初创公司Nucleus的股份，Nucleus为家庭制造了一种视频通信设备。8个月后，在获得Nucleus的计划和财务信息后，亚马逊宣布推出Echo Show，这是一款支持Alexa的大视频屏幕设备。据“华尔街日报”报道，Nucleus消费设备的销量迅速下滑，自那以来该公司已转向向医疗保健市场销售。
报道称，亚马逊后来以500万美元与Nucleus达成和解，但没有承认有任何不当行为。
其他公司，如DefinedCrowd，vocalife和Ubi-制造了一款与Echo类似的声控扬声器-也告诉“华尔街日报”，亚马逊已经与他们会面或投资，后来似乎使用了他们的技术。
在许多情况下，规模较小的公司表示，他们不能再在这个领域竞争，并进行了裁员或关闭。
Echo的前身、声控扬声器Ubi的创始人Leor Grebler告诉“华尔街日报”，我们最终耗费了大量资金，最终不得不缩减公司的大部分规模。
亚马逊告诉“商业内幕”，它不使用亚马逊投资创造竞争产品时公司共享的机密信息。
26年来，我们开创了许多功能、产品，甚至是全新的类别。亚马逊的一位发言人告诉“商业内幕”，从亚马逊自己到Kindle、Echo再到AWS，很少有公司在创新方面能与亚马逊匹敌。不幸的是，总会有一些自私自利的人抱怨而不是建设。任何关于知识产权所有权的合法纠纷都会在法庭上得到正确的解决。
“华尔街日报”的报告发布之际，亚马逊正因潜在的反竞争行为而面临审查。众议院司法委员会将于周一举行反垄断听证会，亚马逊首席执行官杰夫·贝佐斯和其他知名科技高管将在听证会上作证。
美国联邦贸易委员会(Federal Trade Commission)也在调查亚马逊和其他科技公司的数百宗收购，以确定它们是否通过收购新兴竞争对手获得了任何不公平的优势。联邦贸易委员会还与亚马逊平台上的卖家进行了交谈，此前“华尔街日报”4月份的一项调查发现，亚马逊利用第三方供应商的数据推出了与之竞争的产品。

note_en:
Amazon has been using the startup investment process to help it make its own competing products, according to a new report from  The Wall Street Journal's Dana Mattioli and Cara Lombardo.
 The Journal spoke with dozens of startup founders, investors, and advisers, who said Amazon met with or invested in their companies, only to later build its own products that directly competed with the smaller company. The Amazon-made products often went on to crush the competition, the Journal found.
 The Journal discovered several examples of Amazon's investments leading to in-house product development. LivingSocial, a deals website, told the Journal that after Amazon took a 30% stake in the company, it began requesting troves of data from the company, hiring away employees, and contacting LivingSocial's clients to offer better deals.
 In another example, investors from the Alexa Fund purchased a stake in the startup Nucleus, which made a video communication device for the home. Eight months later, after gaining access to Nucleus' plans and financials, Amazon announced the Echo Show, an Alexa-enabled device with a large video screen. Sales of Nucleus' consumer device quickly declined and the company has since pivoted to selling to the healthcare market, the Journal reports.
   Amazon later settled with Nucleus for $5 million but did not admit any wrongdoing, according to the report.
 Other companies, like DefinedCrowd, Vocalife, and Ubi — which made a voice-operated speaker similar to the Echo — also told the Journal Amazon had met with them or invested in them, then later seemed to use their technology.
 In many instances, the smaller companies said they could no longer compete in the space, and downsized or closed.
 "We ended up burning through our cash and ended up having to downsize most of the company," Leor Grebler, who created the voice-operated speaker Ubi, an Echo predecessor, told The Wall Street Journal.
   Amazon told Business Insider that it does not use confidential information that companies share when Amazon invests to create competing products.
 "For 26 years, we've pioneered many features, products, and even whole new categories. From amazon.com itself to Kindle to Echo to AWS, few companies can claim a track record for innovation that rivals Amazon's," an Amazon spokesperson told Business Insider. "Unfortunately, there will always be self-interested parties who complain rather than build. Any legitimate disputes about intellectual property ownership are rightly resolved in the courts."
 The Journal's report comes as Amazon is facing scrutiny about potentially anticompetitive behavior. The House Judiciary Committee is  holding an antitrust hearing on Monday at which Amazon CEO Jeff Bezos and other prominent tech executives will testify.
 The Federal Trade Commission is also looking into "hundreds" of acquisitions by Amazon and other tech firms to determine whether they gained any unfair advantages by purchasing "nascent competitors." The FTC has also spoken with sellers on Amazon's platform after  a Journal investigation from April found that Amazon used data from third-party vendors to launch competing products.

title: 据报道，亚马逊在推出竞争对手之前对初创公司进行了投资，并获得了专有信息，在此过程中往往会压垮规模较小的公司。
title_en: Amazon reportedly invested in startups and gained proprietary information before launching competitors, often crushing the smaller companies in the process
